calendar.matters:
Petition Of Guardian Ad Litem For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Costs; And Petition For Discharge Of Guardian Ad Litem
Off Calendar Sep-26-2018 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Termination Of Irrevocable Subtrusts
Off Calendar Jun-07-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Jackson for and with Nicholas Bracco and for Andrew Bracco.
Lawrence Siracusa Guardian Ad Litem.

The Court noted that this is a petition to terminate sub-trusts with consent of all of the Beneficiaries including with Mr. Siracusa any potential unborn Beneficiaries. Mr. Jackson stated that the purpose of the Trust was to provide for the Children, the then living Grandchildren and the Grandchildren that were not alive at creation. Mr. Jackson also stated that when looking at the distributions received, they are relatively small in nature, that the costs of the Trusts are larger than the income, that fees are at $28,000.00, that income is at $18,000.00, that there was a distribution of $50,000.00 and that the fees are feeding into the income. In addition, Mr. Jackson stated that he thinks the Trustors did not expect costs to get this high, that the Settlors didn't necessarily anticipate it, that the intent was to provide for those Grandchildren, that it still can be effectuated, and that they are all adults and can handle their finances. Mr. Siracusa stated that he is in support of this, that the exercise by the Beneficiaries of their powers of appointment cuts off any interest the unborn issue have, spoke of the costs of the Trust, stated that if the two Beneficiaries do have Children presumably they will provide for their Children, and that there are two other Trusts that will not be terminated. The Court questions why Kathy's Trust is not being terminated. Mr. Jackson stated that the Petitioners are not Beneficiaries of Kathy's Trust, that this Trust may have been a tax savings tool, and that the Trustee is not going to contest or object. Mr. Jackson provided a revised order which allows the Trustee after termination to make distribution in kind. The Petition for Termination of Irrevocable Subtrusts is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
